2026-04-21T18:53:40.1061331Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T18:53:40.1061883Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T18:53:40.1062317Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T18:53:40.1062680Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T18:53:40.1062988Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T18:53:40.1063224Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T18:53:40.1063381Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-21T18:53:40.1064715Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-21T18:55:00.5554522Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:54:45.223135888Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58455","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-21T18:55:00.5579285Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:54:45.478393257Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58455,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Extension List-op method names on Mockable*Resource / {Service}Extensions are hardcoded, ignore @@clientName on the operation\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nIn the new mgmt generator (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`), extension method names for resource operations on `Mockable*Resource` and `{Service}Extensions` are produced by a hardcoded switch on `ResourceOperationKind`. The operation\\u0026#39;s own spec-side `@@clientName` is **not consulted**, so there is no spec-side lever to rename the generated extension method for a List (or Read/Create/Delete/Update) op.\\n\\n## Source\\n\\n`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Utilities/ResourceHelpers.cs:61-92`\\n\\n`csharp\\npublic static string? GetExtensionOperationMethodName(\\n    ResourceOperationKind operationKind, string resourceName, bool isAsync)\\n{\\n    return operationKind switch\\n    {\\n        ResourceOperationKind.List   =\\u0026gt; isAsync ? $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Get{resourceName.Pluralize()}Async\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n                                                : $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Get{resourceName.Pluralize()}\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n        ResourceOperationKind.Read   =\\u0026gt; isAsync ? $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Get{resourceName}Async\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n                                                : $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Get{resourceName}\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n        ResourceOperationKind.Create =\\u0026gt; isAsync ? $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;CreateOrUpdate{resourceName}Async\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n                                                : $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;CreateOrUpdate{resourceName}\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n        ResourceOperationKind.Delete =\\u0026gt; isAsync ? $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Delete{resourceName}Async\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n                                                : $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Delete{resourceName}\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n        ResourceOperationKind.Update =\\u0026gt; isAsync ? $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Update{resourceName}Async\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n                                                : $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Update{resourceName}\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n        _ =\\u0026gt; null\\n    };\\n}\\n`\\n\\n`resourceName` is the resource model name (after any model-level `@@clientName`), pluralized for List ops. The op\\u0026#39;s own `@@clientName` is never read here. The sibling `GetOperationMethodName` (around line 61) used for `ResourceClientProvider` / `ResourceCollectionClientProvider` is equally hardcoded (list on a `*Collection` is always `GetAll[Async]`).\\n\\n## Repro\\n\\nBrownfield SDK with baseline autorest-emitted name `GetFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPoliciesByFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicy[Async]` on `SubscriptionResource` (via `MockableFrontDoorSubscriptionResource` / `FrontDoorExtensions`).\\n\\nSpec (`client.tsp`):\\n\\n`\\tsp\\n@@clientName(WebApplicationFirewallPolicies.listBySubscription,\\n  \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;GetFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPoliciesByFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicy\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n  \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n`\\n\\nAfter regen:\\n\\n- `MockableFrontDoorSubscriptionResource.GetFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicies[Async]` — **unchanged** (still short).\\n- `FrontDoorExtensions.GetFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicies[Async]` — **unchanged**.\\n- Internal pageable collection-result type `PoliciesGetByFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPoliciesCollectionResultOf` — **does** get renamed from the op `@@clientName`.\\n\\nSo the op-level clientName is partially applied: it moves downstream artifact names, but not the extension-method name that actually appears on the public surface. This is confusing and makes ApiCompat remediation for brownfield SDKs require customized-code workarounds (`[CodeGenSuppress]` + reimplementation, or `[CodeGenMember]` rename) for what should be a one-line spec change.\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\n- Any brownfield migration whose baseline exposed a non-default List/Read/Update/etc. extension-method name has to fall back to `Customized/` code. Discovered in FrontDoor migration (Azure/azure-sdk-for-net PR #58439).\\n- Half-applied rename (op clientName shows up in internal types but not on the public method) is itself a latent consistency bug.\\n\\n## Suggested fix\\n\\nIn `GetExtensionOperationMethodName` (and its sibling `GetOperationMethodName`), check for an operation-level client name override first and use it when present; fall back to the current hardcoded pattern only when there is no override. The override should come from whichever TCGC/emitter-level API exposes the `@@clientName` on the InputOperation.\\n\\n## Workarounds used today (FrontDoor)\\n\\n1. `[CodeGenSuppress(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, typeof(...))]` + hand-written long-named method that delegates — on both `Mockable*Resource` and `{Service}Extensions`.\\n2. Or `[CodeGenMember(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;)]` to rename the generated method in place.\\n\\nBoth require customized code for something that is purely a naming concern.\\n\\n## References\\n\\n- Source: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Utilities/ResourceHelpers.cs` (`GetOperationMethodName`, `GetExtensionOperationMethodName`).\\n- Observed during Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor TypeSpec migration (PR #58439).\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58455\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"haiyuazhang\",\"id\":1924967,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/haiyuazhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"assignees\":[\"haiyuazhang\",\"Copilot\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":2,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":2},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-21T18:52:11Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-21T18:53:53Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-21T18:55:00.5600265Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:54:45.49318119Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58455,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-21T18:55:00.5624630Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T18:54:45.710230265Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58455,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Extension List-op method names on Mockable*Resource / {Service}Extensions are hardcoded, ignore @@clientName on the operation\",\"body\":\"## Summary\\n\\nIn the new mgmt generator (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`), extension method names for resource operations on `Mockable*Resource` and `{Service}Extensions` are produced by a hardcoded switch on `ResourceOperationKind`. The operation\\u0026#39;s own spec-side `@@clientName` is **not consulted**, so there is no spec-side lever to rename the generated extension method for a List (or Read/Create/Delete/Update) op.\\n\\n## Source\\n\\n`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Utilities/ResourceHelpers.cs:61-92`\\n\\n`csharp\\npublic static string? GetExtensionOperationMethodName(\\n    ResourceOperationKind operationKind, string resourceName, bool isAsync)\\n{\\n    return operationKind switch\\n    {\\n        ResourceOperationKind.List   =\\u0026gt; isAsync ? $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Get{resourceName.Pluralize()}Async\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n                                                : $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Get{resourceName.Pluralize()}\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n        ResourceOperationKind.Read   =\\u0026gt; isAsync ? $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Get{resourceName}Async\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n                                                : $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Get{resourceName}\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n        ResourceOperationKind.Create =\\u0026gt; isAsync ? $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;CreateOrUpdate{resourceName}Async\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n                                                : $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;CreateOrUpdate{resourceName}\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n        ResourceOperationKind.Delete =\\u0026gt; isAsync ? $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Delete{resourceName}Async\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n                                                : $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Delete{resourceName}\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n        ResourceOperationKind.Update =\\u0026gt; isAsync ? $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Update{resourceName}Async\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n                                                : $\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;Update{resourceName}\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n        _ =\\u0026gt; null\\n    };\\n}\\n`\\n\\n`resourceName` is the resource model name (after any model-level `@@clientName`), pluralized for List ops. The op\\u0026#39;s own `@@clientName` is never read here. The sibling `GetOperationMethodName` (around line 61) used for `ResourceClientProvider` / `ResourceCollectionClientProvider` is equally hardcoded (list on a `*Collection` is always `GetAll[Async]`).\\n\\n## Repro\\n\\nBrownfield SDK with baseline autorest-emitted name `GetFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPoliciesByFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicy[Async]` on `SubscriptionResource` (via `MockableFrontDoorSubscriptionResource` / `FrontDoorExtensions`).\\n\\nSpec (`client.tsp`):\\n\\n`\\tsp\\n@@clientName(WebApplicationFirewallPolicies.listBySubscription,\\n  \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;GetFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPoliciesByFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicy\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;,\\n  \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;);\\n`\\n\\nAfter regen:\\n\\n- `MockableFrontDoorSubscriptionResource.GetFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicies[Async]` — **unchanged** (still short).\\n- `FrontDoorExtensions.GetFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPolicies[Async]` — **unchanged**.\\n- Internal pageable collection-result type `PoliciesGetByFrontDoorWebApplicationFirewallPoliciesCollectionResultOf` — **does** get renamed from the op `@@clientName`.\\n\\nSo the op-level clientName is partially applied: it moves downstream artifact names, but not the extension-method name that actually appears on the public surface. This is confusing and makes ApiCompat remediation for brownfield SDKs require customized-code workarounds (`[CodeGenSuppress]` + reimplementation, or `[CodeGenMember]` rename) for what should be a one-line spec change.\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\n- Any brownfield migration whose baseline exposed a non-default List/Read/Update/etc. extension-method name has to fall back to `Customized/` code. Discovered in FrontDoor migration (Azure/azure-sdk-for-net PR #58439).\\n- Half-applied rename (op clientName shows up in internal types but not on the public method) is itself a latent consistency bug.\\n\\n## Suggested fix\\n\\nIn `GetExtensionOperationMethodName` (and its sibling `GetOperationMethodName`), check for an operation-level client name override first and use it when present; fall back to the current hardcoded pattern only when there is no override. The override should come from whichever TCGC/emitter-level API exposes the `@@clientName` on the InputOperation.\\n\\n## Workarounds used today (FrontDoor)\\n\\n1. `[CodeGenSuppress(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;, typeof(...))]` + hand-written long-named method that delegates — on both `Mockable*Resource` and `{Service}Extensions`.\\n2. Or `[CodeGenMember(\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;)]` to rename the generated method in place.\\n\\nBoth require customized code for something that is purely a naming concern.\\n\\n## References\\n\\n- Source: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Utilities/ResourceHelpers.cs` (`GetOperationMethodName`, `GetExtensionOperationMethodName`).\\n- Observed during Azure.ResourceManager.FrontDoor TypeSpec migration (PR #58439).\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58455\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"haiyuazhang\",\"id\":1924967,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/haiyuazhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\",\"CodeGen\"],\"assignees\":[\"haiyuazhang\",\"Copilot\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":2,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":2},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-21T18:52:11Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-21T18:53:53Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
